overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong and highly targeted social media presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a combined following of 3,580. His content focuses on computational social science, disinformation, coordinated behavior, and academic opportunities. Across all platforms, the audience is highly engaged, particularly responding to research breakthroughs, software updates like CooRnet, and academic job openings.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of newly published papers, software updates (CooRnet v2.0), and presentations on detecting coordinated social media campaigns.
Sharing recruitment opportunities for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students alongside major research grant success stories.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high amplification rate to share call-for-applications and recruitment posts, as users there are highly prone to reposting opportunities.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon with technical deep-dives into software tools like CooRnet to engage its highly specialized user base.
Capitalize on LinkedIn's high absolute reach by utilizing rich media or links when announcing major grants to drive professional networking opportunities.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky acts as the most engaging platform by rate (6.14% average), showing an active audience that frequently amplifies content through reposts, especially for research announcements and PhD recruitment.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a highly targeted audience of academic peers who respond strongly to technical software updates like CooRnet, though overall follower count and engagement volume remain lower.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn yields the highest absolute reach and engagement (likes and comments) for professional milestones such as grant announcements and postdoc hiring, leveraging its larger follower base of 1,850.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
